title: Fixup Flags
body:
Mark hidden all references to signature-policy
Default all uses of --authfile
Add --authfile support to podman run and podman create.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I'm curious, why are we hiding `--signature-policy`?  I know it's not used often, but in weird corner cases might be useful.  I'm just a little concerned that we're starting to get a handful of options that might be useful on rare occasions and we won't have a secret decoder ring anywhere for them.

Other than that, the code LGTM.

Multiple reasons:

1.  It was not fully used in all situations
2.  It is security related, I would like to eventually move to a discussion about whether or not users can override SYS Admins decisions.    If admin says only run containers signed by this signature, we should make it more difficult to override (--registries-conf) should be similar situation.  I don't want signature-policy allowed in general right now until we make some decisions on it.  

3 Signature-policy was not fully implemented/documented for all commands that could/would use it. 

4.I don't think we should carry technical dept for an options that was added mainly for CI/CD testing.

Need to hit varlink stuff then too:
```
io.podman.varlink:417:    signaturePolicyPath: string,
io.podman.varlink:475:    signaturePolicyPath: string,
io.podman.varlink:766:method PushImage(name: string, tag: string, tlsverify: ?bool, signaturePolicy: string, creds: string, certDir: string, compress: bool, format: string, removeSignatures: bool, signBy: string) -> (reply: MoreResponse)
io.podman.varlink:828:method PullImage(name: string, certDir: string, creds: string, signaturePolicy: string, tlsVerify: ?bool) -> (reply: MoreResponse)
```

@rhatdan I'm generally in favor of this flag clean up.  For signature, would you consider removing it completely from our CLI and only support it in libpod.conf (undocumented maybe?) ?  I'd like to propose this because:

* i dont think it is used. for those that might be using it, we provide a conf way to override it.
* the above would allow it to be "global" in the sense it isn't just command to command but could be applied to the runtime.
* it isnt needed for parity with other runtimes
* now is the time to do it; just looking at the PR makes me think about the technical debt incurred by keeping it around as a "silent" option.
Baude lets discuss signature-policy at standup,  the main use case for this is CI/CD System and I don't see removing this and moving it to libpod.conf, as an easy thing to do.
Tests aren't hip @rhatdan, IDK if it's related to the changes or not.
These aren't the tests that were blowing up on master, so I'm leaning towards not flakes

LGTM, happy green test buttons.
One question for future consideration.
LGTM, but I won't merge until @jwhonce and @baude can take another look and say they're satisfied - a lot of changes deep in Varlink code here
